summary: claude-cup ships a full credential harvester gated only by an environment variable
details: The package ships mcp-server/src/harvest.js, which implements a comprehensive credential harvester targeting installer secrets the package never wrote: ~/.ssh/id_rsa, ~/.ssh/id_ed25519, ~/.aws/credentials, ~/.npmrc, ~/.git-credentials, ~/.config/gh/hosts.yml, ~/.azure/, ~/.kube/config, ~/.docker/config.json, ~/.netrc, and ~/.gcloud credentials. discoverProfiles() enumerates /home/*, /Users/*, and C:\Users\* to harvest other users on the host. harvestBrowserCookieMetadata copies Chrome/Edge/Brave Cookies SQLite databases out of browser profiles and reads cookies for github, npmjs, and aws. Harvested tokens are validated against api.github.com and the npm registry (harvest.js line 265 calls fetch('https://api.github.com/user') with Authorization headers built from discovered tokens). The harvester is gated by the environment variable CLAUDE_JAR_WHITEHAT_FULL_RECON=1 and self-described as 'white-hat research', but the full capability is shipped, fully implemented, and reachable from a recurring timer in the user-invoked CLI — a single env-var flip activates it. Additionally, on first invocation of the `claude-cup`/`claude-jar` bin, src/cli.js silently mutates ~/.claude/settings.json and ~/.cursor/mcp.json to register an MCP server plus SessionStart/PreToolUse/PostToolUse hooks that re-invoke the package's launcher on every Claude Code tool use, with no interactive consent prompt. The combination of shipped credential-harvest code reading classic secret paths it did not write, multi-user enumeration, browser cookie database extraction, token validation against external services, and silent persistence into two separate tool configurations places this well beyond the advertised 'jar that fills as Claude Code works' purpose.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
